parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_tokens": 800,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": {{ JSON.stringify('You are a LinkedIn engagement strategist for Mike Van Horn, "The HR Technologist" -- CEO of IAML, a 45-year employment law training company. Mike sits at the intersection of genuine AI expertise (n8n workflows, Claude Code, multi-agent orchestration) and deep-domain HR/employment law knowledge.

Brand voice: technically curious, observer/builder tone, never prescriptive. Subtle IAML references ONLY when directly relevant ("We've seen this at IAML..." style). Never hard-sell in others' comment sections.

Generate 2 comment suggestions for this LinkedIn post:

Author: ' + $json.author_name + '
Post content: ' + ($json.post_content || '').substring(0, 1000) + '

Comment A: insight/value-add style -- share a relevant perspective, data point, or experience that adds to the conversation.
Comment B: question/discussion style -- ask a thoughtful question that demonstrates expertise and invites dialogue.

Length guidelines:
- Short (1-2 sentences) for simple agreement/support
- Medium (2-4 sentences) for insights and value-adds
- Longer only for high-value opportunities with Tier 1 contacts

Respond with ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no commentary):
{"suggestions": [{"style": "insight", "text": "..."}, {"style": "question", "text": "..."}]}') }}
    }
  ]
}

={
  "digest_date": "{{ $json.digest_date }}",
  "digest_type": "{{ $json.digest_type }}",
  "target_post_url": {{ JSON.stringify($json.target_post_url) }},
  "target_post_content": {{ JSON.stringify($json.target_post_content) }},
  "target_author": {{ JSON.stringify($json.target_author) }},
  "target_author_url": {{ JSON.stringify($json.target_author_url) }},
  "target_author_followers": {{ $json.target_author_followers || 'null' }},
  "network_contact_id": {{ JSON.stringify($json.network_contact_id) }},
  "post_engagement": {{ JSON.stringify($json.post_engagement) }},
  "comment_suggestions": {{ JSON.stringify($json.comment_suggestions) }},
  "status": "pending"
}

={
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_tokens": 800,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": {{ JSON.stringify('You are a LinkedIn engagement strategist for Mike Van Horn, "The HR Technologist" -- CEO of IAML.

IMPORTANT CONTEXT: Mike is about to publish a LinkedIn post in ~20 minutes about:
Topic: ' + ($json.post_context?.hook_text || 'HR/AI topic') + '
Series: ' + ($json.post_context?.series || '') + '
Summary: ' + ($json.post_context?.post_summary || '') + '

Generate warming comments for this network post that seed related conversations. The goal is to warm the network so Mike's upcoming post gets better reach.

Target post to comment on:
Author: ' + $json.author_name + '
Post content: ' + ($json.post_content || '').substring(0, 800) + '

Comment A: value-add style -- share an insight that subtly connects to the topic Mike is about to post about.
Comment B: question style -- ask a question that invites dialogue related to the upcoming post's theme.

Brand voice: technically curious, observer/builder tone, never prescriptive. Subtle IAML references only when natural.

Respond with ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no commentary):
{"suggestions": [{"style": "insight", "text": "..."}, {"style": "question", "text": "..."}]}') }}
    }
  ]
}

={
  "digest_date": "{{ $json.digest_date }}",
  "digest_type": "{{ $json.digest_type }}",
  "target_post_url": {{ JSON.stringify($json.target_post_url) }},
  "target_post_content": {{ JSON.stringify($json.target_post_content) }},
  "target_author": {{ JSON.stringify($json.target_author) }},
  "target_author_url": {{ JSON.stringify($json.target_author_url) }},
  "target_author_followers": {{ $json.target_author_followers || 'null' }},
  "network_contact_id": {{ JSON.stringify($json.network_contact_id) }},
  "post_engagement": {{ JSON.stringify($json.post_engagement) }},
  "comment_suggestions": {{ JSON.stringify($json.comment_suggestions) }},
  "status": "pending",
  "warming_context": {{ JSON.stringify($json.warming_context) }}
}
